The common successful entry point within all operations was an email message targeted to victim's public-facing services that contained a Microsoft Word document attachment Sending targeted emails containing malicious attachments to gain initial access.

Upon opening the attachment Execution of malicious code when the user opens a file or link.

multiple malicious files were created or downloaded allowing the attackers to gain some level of access into the victim's infrastructure. Transfer of tools or files from an external system to the compromised system.

Figure 4: File process the role of each of the four dropped files

Data from section "last" is written into registry perhaps to keep track of last executed command Modification of registry keys for various malicious purposes, such as keeping track of the last command executed.

persistence was achieved by utilizing scheduled tasks Use of scheduled tasks to maintain persistence in the system.

several of the Operating System's auto-start locations Using automatic operating system startup locations to maintain persistence.

allowed attackers to gain Domain or even Enterprise Admin access Using valid credentials to access various systems.

Cloud services such as Google Docs and Google Forms were involved allowing attackers to keep track of infected systems, spread malware and perform further malicious activities Using web services to store information that threat actors can retrieve.

Cloud services such as Google Docs and Google Forms were involved allowing attackers to keep track of infected systems, spread malware and perform further malicious activities Cloud services were involved

scripting code (PowerShell, JavaScript, VBS) Using command interpreters and scripting to execute code.

Anunak,

What seems to be the core tools of these activities is a variant of Anunak, remote backdoor, along with a Visual Basic Script specially crafted with data exfiltration features Steal data by exfiltrating it over a command and control channel

One other significant finding is that some of the executables were signed using valid certificates from Comodo, a Certification Authority Attackers acquire code signing certificates to sign their malicious executables.
